task.name: Create shared types for event ledger
task.verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` and confirm no type errors.

task.name: Create Zod validation schemas for event ledger
task.action:
Create `src/lib/validations/event-ledger.ts` with three schemas:
1. `eventLedgerQuerySchema` (server-side, for GET API route): - tagIds: z.string().min(1, "At least one tag is required") - startDate: z.string().optional() - endDate: z.string().optional() - accountId: z.string().min(1).optional() - skip: z.coerce.number().int().min(0).default(0) - take: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(50).default(10)
2. `eventLedgerTagSchema` (server-side, for PATCH tag route): - transactionId: z.string().min(1) - transactionType: z.enum(["expense", "income"]) - tagId: z.string().min(1)
3. `eventLedgerFormSchema` (client-side, for React Hook Form): - tagIds: z.array(z.string()).min(1, "Select at least one tag") - startDate: z.date().optional().nullable() - endDate: z.date().optional().nullable() - accountId: z.string().optional()
Export all schemas and their inferred types. Follow the pattern in `src/lib/validations/transaction-analysis.ts`.
task.verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` and confirm no type errors.

task.name: Create event ledger query API route
task.action:
Create `src/app/api/reports/event-ledger/route.ts` with a GET handler.
Follow the auth pattern from `src/app/api/reports/transaction-analysis/route.ts`: - `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` - Import `auth` from `@/lib/auth`, `headers` from `next/headers`, `db` from `@/lib/prisma` - Check session, return 401 if missing - Extract and validate query params using `eventLedgerQuerySchema` - Return 400 on validation failure
Parse `tagIds` by splitting the comma-separated string into an array.
Build shared filter conditions: - `tags: { some: { id: { in: tagIdArray } } }` - Conditionally add: date range (gte/lte), accountId - Always include `userId` from session
Execute in parallel via `Promise.all`:
1. `db.expenseTransaction.aggregate` with `{ where: { userId, isInstallment: false, ...sharedFilters }, _sum: { amount: true }, _count: true }` 2. `db.incomeTransaction.aggregate` with `{ where: { userId, ...sharedFilters }, _sum: { amount: true }, _count: true }` 3. `db.expenseTransaction.findMany` with `{ where: same, orderBy: { date: 'desc' }, take: skip + take + 1, include: { account: { select: { name: true } }, expenseType: { select: { name: true } }, expenseSubcategory: { select: { name: true } }, tags: { select: { id: true, name: true, color: true } } } }` 4. `db.incomeTransaction.findMany` with `{ where: same, orderBy: { date: 'desc' }, take: skip + take + 1, include: { account: { select: { name: true } }, incomeType: { select: { name: true } }, tags: { select: { id: true, name: true, color: true } } } }`
Merge expense and income transaction lists: - Map expenses to `EventLedgerTransaction` with `type: "expense"`, `categoryName` from `expenseType.name`, `subcategoryName` from `expenseSubcategory?.name` - Map income to `EventLedgerTransaction` with `type: "income"`, `categoryName` from `incomeType.name`, no subcategory - Concatenate, sort by `date` descending - Slice from `skip` to `skip + take`, set `hasMore = merged.length > skip + take`
Calculate: - `totalExpenses` = expense aggregate sum (rounded to 2 decimals, default 0) - `totalIncome` = income aggregate sum (rounded to 2 decimals, default 0) - `netAmount` = totalExpenses - totalIncome
Return JSON response. Wrap in try/catch, return 500 on failure.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm the route compiles. Manual test: Start dev server, call `GET /api/reports/event-ledger?tagIds=some-tag-id` with a valid session cookie. Confirm 200 response with correct shape. Confirm 400 when tagIds is empty.

task.name: Create quick-tag API route
task.action:
Create `src/app/api/reports/event-ledger/tag/route.ts` with a PATCH handler.
Auth: same pattern as above.
Parse and validate request body using `eventLedgerTagSchema`.
Based on `transactionType`: - "expense": `db.expenseTransaction.update({ where: { id: transactionId, userId }, data: { tags: { connect: { id: tagId } } } })` - "income": `db.incomeTransaction.update({ where: { id: transactionId, userId }, data: { tags: { connect: { id: tagId } } } })`
Use `tags: { connect: { id: tagId } }` (NOT `set`) to append without removing existing tags.
Return 200 with `{ success: true }`.
Handle Prisma errors: - Record not found â†’ 404 - Tag already connected (unique constraint on implicit join) â†’ return 200 no-op (Prisma `connect` is idempotent, so this should just work) - Other errors â†’ 500
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm the route compiles.

task.name: Create useEventLedger query and mutation hooks
task.action:
Create `src/hooks/useEventLedger.ts`.
Import `useQuery`, `useMutation`, `useQueryClient` from `@tanstack/react-query`. Import types from `@/types/event-ledger`.
1. `fetchEventLedger(params: EventLedgerParams)`: - Build `URLSearchParams`, joining `tagIds` with commas - Fetch `GET /api/reports/event-ledger?${params}` - Throw on non-ok response - Return typed `EventLedgerResponse`
2. Export `useEventLedger(params: EventLedgerParams)`: - `useQuery` with queryKey `['eventLedger', params]`, enabled: false, staleTime: 5 min, refetchOnWindowFocus: false - Return `{ data: data ?? null, isFetching, error, refetch }`
3. `addTagToTransaction(params: EventLedgerTagParams)`: - Fetch `PATCH /api/reports/event-ledger/tag` with JSON body - Throw on non-ok response
4. Export `useEventLedgerTag()`: - `useMutation` calling `addTagToTransaction` - `onSuccess`: invalidate `['eventLedger']` queries via `queryClient.invalidateQueries` - Return `{ addTag: mutate, isAdding: isPending }`
Follow the return pattern from `src/hooks/useTransactionAnalysis.ts`.
task.verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to confirm no type errors.

task.name: Create EventLedger filter form UI
task.action:
Create `src/components/reports/EventLedger.tsx` as a `"use client"` component.
This task creates the FILTER FORM only. Results display is added in the next task.
Component structure: 1. React Hook Form with `zodResolver(eventLedgerFormSchema)`, defaults: `{ tagIds: [], startDate: null, endDate: null, accountId: "" }` 2. Use `useTagsQuery()`, `useAccountsQuery()` for filter data. 3. Use `useEventLedger` hook. 4. Track `hasAnalyzed` state.
Render a Shadcn `Card` with: - `CardHeader`: title "Event Ledger", description "See the real cost of an event by combining expenses and income under the same tags" - `CardContent`
Inside `CardContent`:
a. **Tag selection (primary filter):** - Reuse the Popover + Command + Checkbox multi-select pattern from TransactionAnalyzer's tag filter. - Selected tags shown as colored Badge pills below the trigger. - Label: "Select Tags"
b. **Optional filters row:** `grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-3 md:gap-4`: - Start Date: Popover + Calendar. Label: "From" - End Date: Popover + Calendar. Label: "To" - Account: Select dropdown. Label: "Account". "All accounts" default.
c. **Action buttons:** `flex justify-end gap-2`: - "Clear" Button (ghost variant). Only visible when any filter is active. - "View Ledger" Button (default variant). Disabled when `tagIds` is empty or while fetching. Show Spinner + "Analyzing..." when fetching.
`handleAnalyze()`: build params from form, call refetch, set hasAnalyzed = true. `handleClear()`: reset form, clear results state.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Temporarily import in reports page to verify it renders correctly.

task.name: Add results display to EventLedger
task.action:
Extend `src/components/reports/EventLedger.tsx` to add the results panel below the filter form.
Only shown when `hasAnalyzed === true` AND `data` is non-null.
Wrapped in `rounded-lg bg-muted/20 p-3 md:p-4 space-y-4 md:space-y-6`.
Sections:
1. **Summary cards:** `grid grid-cols-3 gap-3`: - "Expenses" card: total expenses formatted as PHP, `text-red-500` accent - "Income" card: total income formatted as PHP, `text-green-500` accent - "Net Cost"/"Net Gain" card: net amount, red if positive (cost), green if negative (gain) - Each card: `p-3 md:p-4 text-center`, label `text-xs text-muted-foreground`, value `text-lg md:text-xl font-bold`
2. **Summary sentence:** `text-sm text-muted-foreground`: - Format: "You spent **â‚±X** and received **â‚±Y** across **N transactions** â€” net cost of **â‚±Z**" - Dynamic values use `font-medium text-foreground` - If net is negative: "net gain of â‚±Z"
3. **Transaction list:** - Each row: flex layout with type indicator (âˆ’ red / + green), name, category info, amount (color-coded), date - Sorted by date descending - "Load More" button with remaining count when `hasMore`
4. **Empty state:** EmptyState widget with SearchX icon when no transactions found.
5. **Loading state:** Skeleton cards (3) + skeleton rows (3) while fetching.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. On dev server: select a tag that has both expense and income transactions. Click "View Ledger". Verify: - Summary cards show correct totals - Net amount is correct (expenses - income) - Transactions are color-coded and sorted by date - Load More works

phase.name: Event Ledger Component â€” Add Transactions

task.name: Add inline transaction search and quick-tag panel
task.action:
Extend `src/components/reports/EventLedger.tsx` to add the "Add Transactions" panel.
Below the transaction list (inside the results panel), add: - An "Add Transactions" button (outline variant, Plus icon) - Clicking it reveals a search panel (use `useState` for open/close)
**Search panel contents:**
1. **Search filters row:** - Type toggle: ToggleGroup "Expense" / "Income" (defaults to "Expense") - Date range: two calendar pickers (From / To) - Category: Select dropdown (dynamic based on type â€” use `useExpenseTypesQuery` or `useIncomeTypesQuery`) - Account: Select dropdown - Name search: Input with search icon - "Search" button to trigger query
2. **Search results:** Reuse `useTransactionAnalysis` hook to query the existing transaction-analysis endpoint.
Display results in a compact list. Each row: - Transaction name, category, account, amount, date - "Add Tag" button (small, outline variant) on the right
Filter out transactions that already have ANY of the currently selected Event Ledger tags. Do this client-side by comparing the transaction IDs against the ledger results.
3. **"Add Tag" action:** - Call `useEventLedgerTag().addTag()` with transactionId, transactionType, and the FIRST tag from the selected Event Ledger tags - On success: the `['eventLedger']` query is invalidated (handled by the mutation hook), so results refresh automatically - Remove the tagged transaction from search results (client-side filter)
4. **Close panel:** "Done" button or X icon to collapse.
Important implementation notes: - The search panel has its OWN form state separate from the main Event Ledger form - Use a separate `useTransactionAnalysis` instance for search results - The TransactionAnalyzer's existing endpoint returns individual transactions â€” reuse it directly
task.verify: On dev server: 1. View ledger for a tag. Click "Add Transactions". 2. Search for a transaction not yet tagged. Verify it appears in results. 3. Click "Add Tag". Verify the tag is added and the transaction moves to the ledger. 4. Verify ledger totals update after adding. 5. Verify the added transaction no longer appears in search results. 6. Click "Done" to close the panel.

task.name: Integrate EventLedger into the reports page
task.action:
Modify `src/app/reports/page.tsx`:
1. Add import: `import EventLedger from '@/components/reports/EventLedger'` (or named import). 2. Inside the page's container, add `<EventLedger />` ABOVE `<TransactionAnalyzer />` (but below `<AnnualSummaryTable />`).
The component is self-contained, so no props are needed.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. On dev server: navigate to /reports. Event Ledger card should appear between Annual Summary and Transaction Analyzer.

task.name: Run full lint, build, and manual verification
task.action:
Run `npm run lint` and fix any errors. Run `npm run build` and ensure zero errors.
Manual verification checklist (from spec section 6.2): 1. Event Ledger visible on reports page above Transaction Analyzer. 2. "View Ledger" disabled without tags selected. 3. Single tag selection shows correct results. 4. Multi-tag selection uses OR logic. 5. Date range and account filters work. 6. Summary cards show correct totals with proper color coding. 7. Net Amount shows "Net Cost" (red) or "Net Gain" (green) correctly. 8. Transactions are color-coded and sorted by date. 9. "Add Transactions" panel opens, search works. 10. Quick-tag adds tag and refreshes ledger. 11. Tagged transaction removed from search results. 12. Load More pagination works. 13. Empty state renders correctly. 14. Clear filters resets everything. 15. Mobile responsive layout works.
task.verify: Lint and build pass with zero errors. All 15 manual checks pass.

task.name: Generate verification document
task.action: Create `docs/event-ledger-verification.md` documenting: - All files created and modified - Lint and build results - Manual test results for each verification item - Any deviations from the spec and rationale
task.verify: The verification document exists and covers all items.
